observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our previous recommendation that:
a) the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant shall:
i. coordinate and expedite the reconciliation of PPE accounts to ensure that all
material discrepancies are promptly investigated, resolved, and reconciled
with the books of accounts; and
ii. adopt the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances of government agencies.
b) the Municipal General Services Officer shall:
i. ensure the preparation and timely submission of the Inventory and Inspection
Report on Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) to the COA Audit Team.
c) the Municipal Accountant shall:
i. effect necessary adjusting entries to address those expenditures erroneously
charge to expense and incorrect PPE sub-accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to:
i. mark unreleased stale checks as "CANCELLED" and submit them to the
Accounting Office along with the original Disbursement Voucher (DV) and
supporting documents.
ii. prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks (see Annex E) per fund at the
end of the year and submit it to the Accounting Office to recognize the
restoration of the unreleased checks to the cash account.
b) Municipal Accountant to:
i. ensure the timely adjustment of stale checks
• coordinate with the Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the submission
of necessary documents for stale check adjustments, including
disbursement vouchers and supporting records.
• prepare and process adjusting journal entries for all stale checks in
accordance with Section 104 of the Government Accounting
Manual (GAM) Volume I, ensuring that the amounts are properly
reverted to the Cash and Cash Equivalents accounts.
ii. prepare journal entries in compliance with Section 79 of GAM Volume I
to restore the cash equivalent of unreleased checks and recognize the
corresponding liability or payable accounts at year-end;
iii. prioritize the timely preparation of the required monthly bank
reconciliation statements to establish the actual and correct cash-in-bank
balance of the Municipality and adjust all reconciling items determined
during the preparation of the required Monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statements;
iv. exert efforts to locate records pertaining to prior year’s difference of cash
balances arising from unreconciled items and prepare the appropriate
adjusting entries thereto; and
v. coordinate with the banks for the submission of debit and credit advice or
other substitute documents that would give information on the nature of
the transactions that would support their eventual recording in the books.

observations.recommendation:
1. For the Municipal Budget Officer to facilitate increasing of budgeted amount of RPT-Basic in succeeding annual budgets, to reflect actual collections trend recorded and reported by the Treasury Department and likewise to consider corresponding RPT projection duly established annually by the Municipal Assessor.
2. For the Local Finance Committee, together with the Local Chief Executive, to evaluate areas necessitating enhancement and implementation of corresponding mitigating measures.
3. For the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that the recommended increase in budgeted RPT-Basic is reflected in the respective annual budgets of ensuing years before enactment thereof; and to guarantee that the local tax enforcement capacity, collection effectiveness and efficiency, and fund safeguarding effectiveness, among others, are evaluated and enhanced as recommended.
4. For the Municipal Accountant to set-up provisions annually for RPT and SET collections, based on the most realizable estimate of such taxes, upon due consideration of pertinent projections by the Municipal Assessor; to prioritize immediate reconciliation of accounting records on RPT and SET with the related records and report of the Treasury Department; and to ensure that all accounting personnel are supervised and reviewed on a regular basis.
5. For the Municipal Treasurer, in close coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to henceforth ensure reconciliation of related records and reports on RPT and SET between their departments.
6. For the Local Chief Executive to spearhead in optimizing the use and safeguarding of properties and funds and ensure that all applicable laws, rules and regulations are strictly followed within the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer to facilitate increasing of budgeted amount of RPT-Basic in succeeding annual budgets to reflect actual collections trend and to consider corresponding RPT projection duly established by the Municipal Assessor. The Local Finance Committee, together with the Local Chief Executive, will evaluate areas necessitating enhancement and implement mitigating measures. The Sangguniang Bayan to ensure the recommended increase in budgeted RPT-Basic is reflected in the annual budgets. The Municipal Accountant to prioritize immediate reconciliation of accounting records on RPT and SET with the related records and report of the Treasury Department. The Municipal Treasurer in close coordination with the Municipal Accountant to ensure reconciliation between departments. The Local Chief Executive to spearhead optimizing use and safeguarding of properties and funds.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still ongoing. Committed to implement recommendations henceforth. Some actions are on-going and being implemented; refer to Part II.

observations.recommendation:
The MPOC, as headed by the Municipal Mayor, to:
i. formulate a 3-year, term-based, Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan in accordance with DILG MC No. 2015-128, and updating thereof, when necessary; and
ii. regularly monitor implementation of POPS PPAs in line with the plan.
The LDC, as headed by the Municipal Mayor, to:
i. update as necessary the AIP of the LGU;
ii. ensure that the POPS Plan, among all other LGU plans, are integrated properly in the AIP in accordance with RA 7160; and
iii. regularly monitor implementation of LGU PPAs in line with the AIP as well as the other pertinent development plans.
The Municipal Mayor to:
i. as head of the MPOC, ensure that the plans and PPAs of the Local Government relevant to peace and order and public safety are in line with the advocacies of the National Peace and Order Council; and
ii. ensure that all applicable laws, rules and regulations in cash management are steadfastly observed across the Municipal Government to safeguard the funds of the LGU and likewise facilitate effectiveness and efficiency in the attainment of its developmental objectives.
observations.managementAction: Adhere with the audit recommendation: formulate and update plans and ensure integration in the AIP; regularly monitor implementation of PPAs; ensure compliance with cash management laws and regulations.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant and the Head of GSO reconcile the differences between the Road Network items recognized in the books and those included in the RPCPPE and thereafter effect the necessary adjustment accordingly; and that the Head of Agency ensure that all related records and reports of the LGU are prepared and issued through well-coordinated information across all pertinent departments, thereby promoting fairness in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Head of GSO reconciled the differences between the Road Network items recognized in the books and those included in the RPCPPE and effected the necessary adjustments. The Head of Agency ensured coordination among departments to prepare and issue related records and reports.

observations.recommendation:

2. That the Local Chief Executive ensure implementation of all identified PPAs in accordance with the approved appropriation/budget.

4. The Local Chief Executive, as primarily accountable for all LGU funds and
properties, to spearhead in the proper management thereof, and ensure that
all applicable laws, rules and regulations appurtenant to operation and
financial reporting are steadfastly observed by all LGU officials and employees
to safeguard LGU funds and foster fairness in financial reporting.

With the above observations, we recommended and the management agreed
on the following:
1. The Municipal Accountant to:
a. expedite liquidation with respect to outstanding cash advances for
Confidential Fund granted in CY 2020 and thereafter effect the
liquidation thereof by derecognizing in the books the corresponding
amount upon submission of liquidation report in accordance with COA-
DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8,
2015;
b. in coordination with Mayor Dibu S. Tuan and Honeylyn Zabate with cash
advances for Confidential Fund cited in COA-ICFAU AOMs, expedite
compliance with required supporting documents and make representation
with the ICFAU for immediate settlement of noted deficiencies;
c. substantiate and provide the audit team of the basis for the liquidation
within the specific periods of the noted restored confidential funds. If
documents found appropriate, drop the latter from books while ensuring
the immediate settlement of AOMs thereof issued by COA-ICFAU.
Henceforth, restore the duly liquidated confidential fund only in case of ND
issued by COA-ICFAU for proper accounting thereof;
d. stop the granting of cash advances to accountable officers/employees
beyond their approved maximum accountability for purposes other than
their official travelling expenses; and
e. stop the practice of recording cash advances directly to expense accounts
and ensure that all cash advances and eventual liquidation thereof are
reported and monitored completely and comprehensibly in the Subsidiary
Ledger for specific “advances accounts” and duly supported with SUCA.
2. The Local Chief Executive, as the primary responsible official of the LGU, to
spearhead and enforce strict adherence with the prescribed laws, rules and
regulations in the granting, utilization, liquidation and recording of cash
advances in order to safeguard the funds of the local government.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive to direct the Heads of the implementing
departments (Mun. Agriculture Office, Mun. Tourism Office, Mun. Health Office,
etc.), in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to coordinate with source
agencies for the substantiation of lacking intended purpose/program of funds
received, implement promptly the corresponding PPAs funded by unutilized
grants, and ensure timely liquidation thereof.

With the foregoing considerations, we recommended and the Local Chief
Executive, together with the Sangguniang Bayan and responsible departments agreed
on the following:
1. Cause the preparation of a justification on the deficiencies noted herein and
provide explanation on why the subject payment of Employees Emergency
Assistance should not be disallowed in audit;
2. Henceforth, refrain from granting any bonuses, allowances and benefits to
individuals in the absence of sufficient legal or statutory basis to avoid audit
suspensions/disallowances in the future.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management
agreed that the Local Chief Executive, with the assistance of the Municipal DRRM
Officer, to regularly enforce and monitor implementation of DRRM-PPAs in order
to ensure that LDRRMF are optimally utilized.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant should review and analyze all prior years and current year’s transactions involving reciprocal accounts and effect necessary adjustments, if any, in the respective books of accounts to ensure that all financial transactions are properly taken up in the books.
The Municipal Accountant should periodically review and analyze transactions involving reciprocal accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management should be vigilant and strictly adhere to the provisions of National Budget Circular No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 regarding procurement of second-hand vehicles so as to avoid wastage of government funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the implementing agency to adhere to the Peace and Order and Public Safety Plan Matrix in appropriating funds for Peace and Order to have a measurable result.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Require all department heads to adhere to regulations pertaining to payment out of the Petty Cash Fund and to follow also the provisions of RA 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act.
Require Municipal Accountant to exercise religiously her mandated functions not only in accounting but also in the internal audit services of the municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the countersigning of checks in payment of obligation should be delegated by the Local Chief Executive to the appointed Municipal Administrator except for check disbursements pertaining to the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan and Office of the Vice Mayor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials be instructed sternly to submit the required financial reports and accounts within the reglementary period.

Recommendation:
Secure the ownership of the Land by working on transferring the title to the Barangay Government of Laconon.

Recommendations:
Appropriate enough funds for minor repairs of the BHS to restore and maintain its good condition before the major renovation of the entire building, when funds are available.
Repaint the Barangay Health Station.
Ask financial assistance from the Municipal Government, Department of Health or other agencies/organizations for the purpose of renovating the Barangay Health Station.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Construct two (2) comfort rooms for male and female patients and provide private area for breastfeeding.
Provide more seating spaces for the patients by having additional chairs and/or expand the waiting shed.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Provide additional cabinets for the BHS to segregate the storage space for supplies, drugs and medicines.
Transfer the examination room to another room far from the lobby and receiving area to ensure privacy of the patients.
Install additional lightings especially in the pre-natal room and comfort rooms.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Require the BHS personnel to post the complete health services together with their availability in a visible signboard or readable by the clients and service providers within the BHS compound and other conspicuous places in the barangay.
Require the BHS personnel to update the staff whereabouts board from time to time.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the MHO and the barangay concerned should provide the required equipment needed by the BHS to effectively and efficiently deliver its services.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Barangay Government of Laconon and Municipality of Tboli should appropriate enough funds for drugs and medicines and supplies necessary for proper implementation of the health programs.
Prepare and post case management charts for ARI, CDD and other programs.
Establish a separate corner within the BHS for functional Oral Rehydration Therapy (ORT).
The Municipal Health Officer should instruct the Midwife assigned in the barangay health center to maintain Updated target Client List (UTCL) or logbook of clients for each program.
Provide IEC materials in order to give information to the public.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the BHS midwife to properly dispose immediately those drugs and medicines which had already expired.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer should prepare monthly schedules of regular visits of the physician and medical technologist and post it in the BHS’s signboards to inform the constituents of different barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer should create a team to deliver regular dental services to the constituents of Barangay Laconon. The Municipal Dentist should have a schedule plan that should be posted in the BHS.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Comply with the provisions of R.A. 7883 by providing the required incentives and benefits to the BHS personnel.
Provide adequate/regular trainings to the BHS personnel especially those trainings that are work-related.

Recommendations:
Require the barangay officials, BHS personnel and municipal rural health unit to strengthen its campaign to inform the entire barangay constituents that the lying-in is already operating and basic facilities are available
Post the schedule of facility hours or the availability of midwives. Also, distribute brochures or IEC materials regarding the operation of lying-in, especially in Sitios that are remote from the lying in.
Provide continuous training to the personnel.
Provide additional delivery beds to accommodate more patients.
Have the Lying-in accredited with the Philhealth to attract Philhealth beneficiaries to deliver in the Barangay Lying In.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Require the Barangay Health Station to segregate the wastes properly and dispose those wastes regularly.
Properly identify the containers by either labelling or color-coding, so that the staff and patients could throw their garbage accordingly.
Provide and use new garbage containers instead of sacks.

Create an Inventory Team that will conduct a complete physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality to ascertain the existence, ownership and validity of the PPE accounts. The Inventory Team, upon completion of inventory should prepare the prescribed RCPPE, and reconcile it with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Conduct a complete physical count. Prepare RCPPE and reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.reason: On-going physical count of PPE by GSO and Accounting.
